text.title: Puppeteer Stealth & Proxy Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Script asserts navigator.webdriver === false; assertion sometimes passes, sometimes fails depending on plugin load order.
antipattern.root-cause: puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth + manual override both touch navigator.webdriver; one overwrites the other unpredictably.
antipattern.fix: Remove the manual override. Document at top of file: 'STEALTH STANCE: plugin-only'.

antipattern.symptom: Security audit finds proxy username + password in CI logs (ps output captured).
antipattern.fix: Use --proxy-server=http://host (no creds) + page.authenticate({username, password}) per page.

antipattern.symptom: Job ran for 6 hours, hit 429 thousands of times, eventually got IP-banned.
antipattern.root-cause: Retry loop with no max; circuit breaker absent.
antipattern.fix: Cap retries at 2 with exponential backoff. Trip a circuit breaker on persistent 4xx and exit non-zero with status.

antipattern.symptom: Random jobs stall for 30s then fail with TLS errors; pattern correlates with specific proxy IPs.
antipattern.root-cause: Dead proxies still in rotation; no health check.
antipattern.fix: HEAD the health endpoint per proxy on pool refresh; remove unhealthy entries; alert ops below pool floor.

antipattern.symptom: Test that asserts navigator.plugins.length > 0 passes on bare Chrome, fails with stealth plugin loaded.
antipattern.root-cause: Assertion against a property the stealth stance overrides.
antipattern.fix: Drop the assertion or run the assertion stage with stealth disabled; never depend on overridden properties for verification.
